extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won Stage 4 sprint after peloton reeled in break on descent, launching center-road while COQUARD pulled alone with no leadout support.
tactical_patterns: COQUARD pulled through final kilometers without leadout, fatal error leaving him exposed for Groves/Strong counterattack in final 200m.
INEOS pushed pace entire day for HAYTER but abandoned him in finale, revealing team coordination breakdown on sprint stages.
Breakaway with CICCONE/DE LA CRUZ/PETERS/JUUL-JENSEN held 30-second gap with 4km remaining, descents killed their timing advantage early.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: Perfect wheel-surfing discipline, launched only after COQUARD exhausted himself pulling, confirms he needs chaos not trains
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: negative: Pulled alone with 4km remaining without leadout, tactical naivety cost him certain victory despite strongest positioning
rider_signals.STRONG Corbin: positive: Took barrier-side risk in technical finale for third, shows ISR willing to gamble without pure sprint train
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: INEOS worked entire stage for him but disappeared in finale, questions team's sprint stage execution capacity
rider_signals.SCHELLING Ide: neutral: Fourth without dedicated leadout suggests Alpecin miscalculated bringing him over pure climbers for this route
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Considered sprinting with 4km left but chose conservation, confirms GC priority over opportunistic stage hunting
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Guided EVENEPOEL personally through finale rather than using teammates, protecting time gaps over stage ambitions
